title: Fix httpx not following redirects for fused-memory MCP calls
analysis: The dashboard shows 'Fused Memory Server Offline' because httpx.AsyncClient() in app.py line 57 defaults to follow_redirects=False. When the fused-memory MCP server's Starlette routing normalizes trailing slashes (307 redirect from /mcp/ to /mcp), mcp_tool_call in memory.py sees a 307 status and raises ValueError('MCP call get_status returned non-200 status: 307'). Two fixes needed: (1) Remove the trailing slash from the URL in memory.py line 34 — change f'{base_url}/mcp/' to f'{base_url}/mcp' to avoid the redirect entirely, and (2) Add follow_redirects=True to the httpx.AsyncClient in app.py line 57 as a safety net against future URL mismatches. The fused-memory server uses MCP SDK's run_streamable_http_async() which uses Starlette routing — Starlette's default redirect_slashes=True sends 307 from /mcp/ to /mcp. Existing test suite uses httpx.MockTransport pattern extensively. The test_successful_call test exercises mcp_tool_call but doesn't verify the URL path sent. The client fixture in conftest.py uses Starlette TestClient which triggers the app lifespan, so we can inspect app.state.http_client to verify follow_redirects.

steps.description: Write failing test in test_memory.py TestMcpToolCall: test_posts_to_correct_url_path — use MockTransport handler to capture the request URL and assert the path is '/mcp' (no trailing slash). Handler inspects request.url.path and returns a valid MCP response. Currently fails because memory.py posts to '{base_url}/mcp/' with trailing slash.

Fix memory.py line 34: change f'{base_url}/mcp/' to f'{base_url}/mcp' (remove trailing slash). Also update the docstring on line 25 that says 'Posts to {base_url}/mcp/' to say 'Posts to {base_url}/mcp'.

Write failing test in test_app.py: test_lifespan_sets_follow_redirects — use the TestClient (which triggers lifespan), access app.state.http_client via the app reference, and assert the client is configured with follow_redirects=True. Currently fails because app.py creates httpx.AsyncClient() without follow_redirects=True.
Fix app.py line 57: change httpx.AsyncClient() to httpx.AsyncClient(follow_redirects=True).

Write regression test in test_memory.py TestMcpToolCall: test_307_redirect_followed_with_follow_redirects — MockTransport handler returns 307 with Location header when path is '/mcp/' and a valid MCP response when path is '/mcp'. Client created with follow_redirects=True. Asserts mcp_tool_call succeeds and returns the expected dict. Documents the original bug scenario and verifies the safety net works.
No new production code needed — step 5 test passes with fixes from steps 2 and 4. Run full test suite (pytest dashboard/tests/) to confirm no regressions across all existing tests.

design_decisions.decision: Apply both URL fix and follow_redirects=True (defense in depth)
design_decisions.rationale: Removing the trailing slash from the URL avoids the redirect overhead and latency entirely. follow_redirects=True is a safety net against future URL mismatches or server routing changes. The task description explicitly requests both fixes.

The MCP SDK's run_streamable_http_async() uses Starlette routing which by default has redirect_slashes=True, redirecting '/mcp/' to '/mcp' via 307. The server log at main.py:156 displays '/mcp/' but the canonical Starlette route is '/mcp'. The 307 error in production confirms this.

design_decisions.decision: Test URL path via MockTransport request inspection
design_decisions.rationale: The existing test suite consistently uses httpx.MockTransport. Inspecting the request.url.path inside the handler is the most direct way to verify the URL construction without changing test infrastructure or needing a live server.

design_decisions.decision: Test follow_redirects via app.state.http_client attribute inspection
design_decisions.rationale: The TestClient fixture triggers the lifespan which creates the http_client on app.state. Inspecting the client object directly verifies the configuration was applied. This is simpler and more reliable than trying to trigger a redirect through the TestClient.

reuse.how: Reuse for building mock MCP responses in the URL path test (step 1) and regression test (step 5)
Same pattern for all new tests — define a handler function, create MockTransport, create AsyncClient with transport. Steps 1 and 5 follow this exact pattern.
Used in step 3 to test that the lifespan-created httpx.AsyncClient has follow_redirects=True. The fixture triggers the full lifespan context manager.
New tests in steps 1 and 5 are added to this existing class, keeping the URL path and redirect tests co-located with other mcp_tool_call behavior tests.
